title: Dragonbane RPG from the solo perspective - Overview / Review / Solo setup
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SHzg-IgnKuI

Daniel (@dungeondive) reviews Free League's *Dragonbane* box set from the solo angle. The system is roll-under-d20 across six attributes, classless with kin-and-profession backgrounds, no XP — skills advance when ticked from critical successes or failures. Combat is fast and brutal: a great sword can one-shot a starting character, but Rally and death rolls let you keep fighting at zero HP, and the deck of improvised-weapon cards (one per environment) gives every fight some surprise. Initiative is dealt fresh each round from a card deck. There are three schools of magic but Daniel uses none of them.

Solo play comes with its own booklet (*Alone in Deepfall Breach*), where your character is treated as an army of one — two initiative cards per round, and the ability to push rolls for 3 willpower without taking a condition. The included map, oracle tables, journey charts, and an adventure book full of location-specific encounter tables all do useful soloist work.

Criticisms: the soloist's tables are scattered across three books, there are no random-monster-by-threat tables, and the loot system is bafflingly mundane — no magic weapons, no GM rules for creating any. Daniel's own campaign hangs a slow-burn volcano usage-die over Magnar Woods as a ticking-clock Omen.

----

Dragonbane gives you Rally, death rolls, and double initiative as a soloist — does the system feel heroic in play, or is it still squishier than the marketing suggests?
